title: ophrys scolopax subsp. oestrifera
intro: Ophrys scolopax subsp. oestrifera, also known as the fly orchid, is a type of orchid that belongs to the Ophrys genus and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The fly orchid is said to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
